mappings.rationaleAI: Covers personality theories, individual differences, abnormal psychology, and the broad framework for personality and mental disorders.
Freud developed psychoanalytic personality theory including id, ego, superego, defense mechanisms, and psychosexual development (A.2, C.3).
Directly covers psychoanalytic theories of personality including the unconscious, Oedipus complex, and life/death instincts (A.2).
Jung's collective unconscious, archetypes, and psychological types are specifically referenced in psychoanalytic personality theories (A.2).
Directly covers the subjective aspect of personality and development of awareness of self (C.1).
Covers identity formation and self-awareness, relevant to the subjective aspect of personality and social analytic theories (C.1, A.3).
Covers authenticity, individuality, and responsibility, relevant to self-awareness and personality functioning (C.1, C.3).
Covers assessment and treatment of mental disorders, including cognitive therapy, personality measurement, and PTSD (B, D).
Directly covers major categories of mental disorders including their causation, genetics, and community treatment (D.1, D.3).
Covers treatment approaches for personality disturbances and mental disorders including behaviour therapy and psychoanalysis (D).
A major therapeutic approach for anxiety disorders, depression, and obsessive-compulsive disorders (D.1).
Schizophrenia is specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (D.1).
Covers depression, bipolar disorder, and manic depression -- affective disorders named as a major category (D.1).
Anxiety disorders are specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (D.1).
Covers the history of mental illness, insanity, and cultural understanding of mental disorders, relevant to prevalence and causation (D.2, D.3).
Autism is a disorder of infancy or childhood, specifically named among major categories of mental disorders (D.1).
Personality disorders are specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (D.1).
Covers substance-related disorders relevant to personality disturbances and defense mechanisms in coping (C.3, D.1).
Organic mental disorders are specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (D.1).
Psychosexual disorders are specifically named as a major category of mental disorder (D.1).
Covers personality assessment in applied contexts, including the thematic apperception test and mental disorder evaluation (B, D.1).
Covers the neurological basis of personality, relevant to physiological theories and organic mental disorders (A.1, D.1).
Covers social influences on personality functioning, relevant to social analytic theories and social stresses on adjustment (A.3, C.2).
Covers personality, well-being, and depression, relevant to personality functioning and adjustment (C).
Directly relevant to prevalence of mental disorders and epidemiological methods (D.2).
Covers physiological mechanisms relevant to physiological theories of personality based on body humours and somatotypes (A.1).
